skill.name: firestore-security-rules-auditor
skill.description: Audits Firebase Firestore security rules for authorization gaps, validation bypasses, privilege risks, and rule design weaknesses.
skill.summary: Reviews Firestore security rules against a focused security checklist for access control and data validation.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but all observed evidence is Markdown rubric text in SKILL.md. No executable code, shell execution, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported three duplicate external command findings on line 19. The line uses Markdown backticks around Firestore helper names such as hasOnly() and diff(); it is not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is inline Markdown formatting in SKILL.md. No script, command runner, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm findings on lines 3 and 41. These lines contain descriptive prose and an output field label, not hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic algorithm, library call, or security-sensitive implementation at the flagged lines. The findings appear to be text-pattern matches.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Audit Checklist
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system reconnaissance on lines 14 and 19. These lines describe Firestore rule audit concepts and do not collect host, user, process, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is a checklist for reviewing Firestore authorization logic. There is no code or instruction to inspect the local system.

content.value_statement: Firestore rules can look strict while still allowing privilege escalation, invalid updates, or data exposure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a focused security review of rule behavior.
actual_capabilities: Reviews create and update rules for validation bypasses.
Checks whether sensitive authority comes from trusted sources.
Identifies ownership and field-level authorization gaps.
Rates findings with a simple one-to-five security score.
Produces remediation guidance for each identified rule weakness.
limitations: Requires the user to provide the relevant Firestore rules and app context.
Does not execute Firebase emulator tests or prove runtime behavior.
Does not replace manual review by a Firebase security specialist.
May miss risks that depend on unseen application code or IAM settings.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Rule Review
use_cases.description: Audit new or changed Firestore rules before deploying them to production.

use_cases.title: Security Regression Check
use_cases.description: Compare rule changes for new update bypasses, ownership gaps, or data validation regressions.

use_cases.title: Design Validation
use_cases.description: Check whether rules support collaboration, admin access, and data boundaries without insecure workarounds.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Rules Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer wants a first review of one rules file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these Firestore security rules. Score them from 1 to 5, summarize the main risk, and list concrete fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Update Bypass Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A team changed validation rules and wants targeted review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Focus on create versus update behavior. Find cases where a user can create valid data and later update it into an invalid or privileged state.

prompt_templates.title: Authorization Source Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A product uses roles, owners, or admin fields.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review whether these rules trust user-controlled fields for role, owner, or admin decisions. Explain each authority source and recommend safer checks.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Threat Model
prompt_templates.scenario: A security reviewer needs a deeper assessment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a red-team review of these Firestore rules using the full checklist. Include bypass paths, business logic gaps, severity, and prioritized remediation steps.

output_examples.input: A ruleset lets authenticated users update profile documents with only field-name restrictions.
output_examples.output: The review flags a major ownership gap because field restrictions do not prove who may update the document. It recommends adding an owner check before allowing updates.

output_examples.input: A ruleset validates field types on create but performs fewer checks on update.
output_examples.output: The review identifies an update bypass and explains how valid documents can become invalid later. It recommends matching update validation to create validation.

output_examples.input: A ruleset grants admin access by matching one verified email address.
output_examples.output: The review accepts the bootstrap pattern when email verification is required and no rule lets users add themselves as admins.

best_practices: Provide the complete rules file and a short description of expected user roles.
Ask for create, update, read, and delete behavior to be reviewed separately.
Validate important fixes with Firebase emulator tests after the review.
anti_patterns: Do not audit isolated rule fragments without the matching data model.
Do not treat field allowlists as ownership checks.
Do not ignore update rules after create validation passes.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews Firestore security rules for access control, validation consistency, privilege escalation, and insecure business logic.

faq.question: Does it run Firebase tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured review and recommendations, but it does not execute emulator or integration tests.

faq.question: Can it review partial rules?
faq.answer: Yes, but complete rules and app context produce more reliable findings.

faq.question: What score does it return?
faq.answer: It uses a one-to-five score where one is critical risk and five means strong rule coverage.

faq.question: Can it find update bypasses?
faq.answer: Yes. The checklist explicitly compares create and update behavior for validation gaps.

faq.question: Is this only for Firestore?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is tailored to Firebase Firestore security rules.
